IDirect3DDevice8::DrawVertices

Renders a sequence of nonindexed, geometric primitives of the specified type from the current set of data input streams. 

HRESULT DrawVertices(
  D3DPRIMITIVETYPE PrimitiveType,
  UINT StartVertex,
  UINT VertexCount,
);

Parameters

PrimitiveType
[in] Member of the D3DPRIMITIVETYPE enumerated type, describing the type of primitive to render.
StartVertex
[in] Index of the first vertex to load. Beginning at StartVertex, the correct number of vertices will be read out of the vertex buffer.
VertexCount
[in] Number of vertices to render. The maximum number of primitives allowed is 32 million. VertexCount is the number of vertices as determined by the primitive type. If it is a line list, each primitive has two vertices. If it is a triangle list, each primitive has three vertices.

Return Values

If the method succeeds, the return value is D3D_OK.

If the method fails, the return value can be D3DERR_INVALIDCALL.

Remarks

This method is identical to IDirect3DDevice8::DrawPrimitive, with the exception that the number of vertices is included in the call rather than the number of primitives. DrawVertices is called by IDirect3DDevice8::DrawPrimitive as part of the rendering process. DrawVertices should not be called with a single triangle at a time.

Requirements

  Header: Declared in D3d8.h.
  Import Library: Use D3d8.lib.

See Also

IDirect3DDevice8::DrawPrimitive, IDirect3DDevice8::DrawIndexedPrimitive, IDirect3DDevice8::SetStreamSource
